questions: rodh e ghumiye porle ki hoy
answers: Heatstroke occurs when body temperature rises above 104°F (40°C). Symptoms include confusion, hot dry skin, rapid heartbeat, and unconsciousness. Move the person to shade, cool them with water, and call 999.
Heatstroke hole matha ghurbe, bomi bomi lagbe, skin dry and hot thakbe. Chaya te niye jan, thanda pani spray korun, fan chalun, ar hospital e niye jan urgently.
To treat heatstroke: Move to a cool place, remove excess clothing, apply ice packs to neck/armpits/groin, give cool water if conscious. This is a medical emergency — call for help immediately.
Heatstroke theke bachte goromer din e pani beshi khaan, dhoop e beshi thakben na, halka colored jama porun, ar baire gele cap/hat porun. Heatstroke serious — deri korle organ damage hote pare.
